rulings.Case Title: IN RE: HAL BARNETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien; Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2023 line 1. PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN; REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS FEES is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Fees granted in the amount of $3,130.10. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MIHAL EMBERTON VS. SAN FRANCISCO CITY GOVERMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to October 20, 2023, for supplemental briefing. Defendant to provide a supplemental brief addressing: 1) under the circumstances of the case and its procedural posture, the avenue for review available to Plaintiff in regards to the proposed rejection of her permit applications from the Department of Public Works on December 10, 2021; and 2) the timeline for the City to issue a final or conditional grant or denial of Plaintiff's permit applications with a date certain for the City's decision, and any steps Plaintiff must take before the City makes the decision, which would render the issue "ripe" for review. Brief not to exceed five pages, due no later than October 13, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HIDEKI B. ITO VS. FUMIKO S. ITO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2023 line 6. PLAINTIFF HIDEKI ITO'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the "no damages" affirmative defense. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the "failure to state a claim" defense as this is not an affirmative defense. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on all other grounds for Defendant to state facts in support of each affirmative defense as to allege specifically "any interests the defendant has or claims in the property." Defendant shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended answer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PIER 39 LIMITED PARTNERSHIP VS. DD RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. DBA LORENZO'S PIZZERIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For (1) Preliminary Injunction, (2) Stay Of Proceedings, And (3) Order Consolidating Cases
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 4, 2023 line 8. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR (1) PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION, (2) STAY OF PROCEEDINGS, AND (3) ORDER CONSOLIDATING CASES is DENIED. Unlawful detainer is a statutorily mandated summary proceeding. The circumstances of this case based on non-payment of rent are not subject to a limited exception to the summary resolution under any of the theories advanced by the moving party. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
